title: Odoo Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Odoo** is an open-source ERP + CRM platform exposing a JSON-RPC 2.0 API at a single endpoint `POST {base_url}/jsonrpc`. This connector — `OdooConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "odoo"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the eight most-used Odoo ORM models plus a generic escape hatch:

| Surface | Odoo model | Methods exposed |
|---|---|---|
| Partners (contacts) | `res.partner` | list / get / create / update / delete |
| CRM | `crm.lead` | list / create |
| Sales | `sale.order` | list |
| Invoices | `account.move` (move_type=out_invoice) | list |
| Products | `product.template` | list |
| Projects | `project.task` | list |
| HR | `hr.employee` | list |
| Inventory | `stock.picking` | list |
| Anything else | any | `execute_method(model, method, args, kwargs)` |

The connector normalizes `res.partner` records into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), funnels every operation through one HTTP client (`client/http_client.py::OdooHTTPClient`), caches the integer `uid` between calls, decodes the error-inside-200 envelopes Odoo uses (SOC — `connector.py` is orchestration only), and retries 503 + transport errors with exponential backoff.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate an Odoo API key

1. Sign in to your Odoo instance (Odoo.com SaaS or self-hosted).
2. Click your avatar → **My Profile** → **Account Security** tab.
3. Under **API Keys** click **New API Key**, give it a memorable name (e.g. `shielva-acp`).
4. Copy the key value — Odoo shows it only once.
5. From the login page note the **Database** name (shown above the Email / Password fields). On Odoo.com SaaS this is usually your subdomain.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Odoo**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `base_url` (text) | `https://mycompany.odoo.com` (no trailing slash needed — connector trims) |
| `db` (text) | Database name from step 5 |
| `username` (text) | Your Odoo login (email) |
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft client cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` calls `common.authenticate(db, username, api_key, {})` — on success Odoo returns the integer `uid` for that session, which proves all four fields are consistent. On failure Odoo returns `false`, which the connector translates to `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which always clears the cached `uid` first, then re-authenticates. Any 401 / AccessDenied / Session-Expired → `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)`. Network errors → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED`. Success → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Odoo's JSON-RPC API uses **per-user API keys** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Why API keys, not passwords

Odoo blocks JSON-RPC logins that present a password when the user has 2FA enabled. API keys bypass 2FA and are explicitly the correct credential for server-to-server use. They were introduced in Odoo 14.

### Credential model

- Every call wraps a JSON-RPC envelope sent to `POST {base_url}/jsonrpc`.
- The `api_key` is placed in the **password slot** of every RPC envelope — there is no `Authorization` header in JSON-RPC.
- The `uid` returned by `common.authenticate(db, login, key, {})` is cached on the client; subsequent `object.execute_kw` calls reuse it until either:
  - `health_check()` runs and explicitly clears the cache, or
  - an `AuthError` / Session-Expired comes back mid-flight (the client clears its own cache before re-raising).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Calls `common.authenticate`. On success → `HEALTHY + AUTHENTICATED` + cached uid. On `false` → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured api_key — surface-compatibility shim. |
| `health_check()` | Clears the uid cache then re-authenticates. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — the `uid` is in-memory only. |

### Envelope

```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method":  "call",
  "params": {
    "service": "common" | "object" | "db",
    "method":  "authenticate" | "execute_kw" | "version" | ...,
    "args":    [db, uid, api_key, model, method, args, kwargs]
  },
  "id": 1
}
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | text | yes | `self.base_url` | Root of the Odoo instance; `/jsonrpc` is appended |
| `db` | text | yes | `self.db` | Database name (login-page label) |
| `username` | text | yes | `self.username` | Odoo user login |
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | Per-user API key (Preferences → Account Security) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

Legacy `instance_url` is also accepted as an alias for `base_url` so older install records keep working.

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["base_url", "db", "username", "api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `OdooConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; the JSON-RPC envelope, retry loop, uid cache and error-inside-200 decoder all live in `client/http_client.py::OdooHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate the four required fields and call `common.authenticate`. Persists config via `save_config` and returns the canonical `ConnectorStatus`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the api_key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Clears the cached uid then re-authenticates so a revoked API key surfaces immediately.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None, page_size=100) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `res.partner` records, normalizes each via `normalize_partner` and hands off through `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult(status=COMPLETED|PARTIAL|FAILED)` with `documents_found / _synced / _failed`.

children.title: Partners (`res.partner`)
children.content:
### `async list_partners(limit=50, offset=0, domain=None, fields=None) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('res.partner', 'search_read', [domain], {limit, offset, fields})`.

### `async get_partner(partner_id, fields=None) -> Dict`
`execute_kw('res.partner', 'read', [[partner_id]], {fields})` — unwraps the single-element list.

### `async create_partner(name, email=None, phone=None, is_company=False) -> int`
`execute_kw('res.partner', 'create', [values])` — returns the new id.

### `async update_partner(partner_id, fields: dict) -> bool`
`execute_kw('res.partner', 'write', [[partner_id], fields])`.

### `async delete_partner(partner_id) -> bool`
`execute_kw('res.partner', 'unlink', [[partner_id]])`.

children.title: CRM · Sales · Invoices
children.content:
### `async list_leads(limit=50, offset=0, domain=None) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('crm.lead', 'search_read', ...)`.

### `async create_lead(name, partner_id=None, email_from=None, expected_revenue=0) -> int`
`execute_kw('crm.lead', 'create', [values])`.

### `async list_sale_orders(limit=50, offset=0, domain=None) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('sale.order', 'search_read', ...)`.

### `async list_invoices(limit=50, offset=0) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('account.move', 'search_read', [[('move_type','=','out_invoice')]], ...)` — customer invoices only.

children.title: Products · Tasks · Employees · Pickings
children.content:
### `async list_products(limit=50, offset=0, domain=None) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('product.template', 'search_read', ...)`.

### `async list_tasks(limit=50, offset=0, domain=None) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('project.task', 'search_read', ...)`.

### `async list_employees(limit=50, offset=0, domain=None) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('hr.employee', 'search_read', ...)`.

### `async list_pickings(limit=50, offset=0, domain=None) -> List[Dict]`
`execute_kw('stock.picking', 'search_read', ...)`.

children.title: Escape Hatch
children.content:
### `async execute_method(model: str, method: str, args=None, kwargs=None) -> Any`
Generic `execute_kw` entry point. Lets callers reach any Odoo model/method (`account.payment`, `hr.leave`, `mail.message`, `mrp.production`, …) without modifying the connector. Adding a domain-specific helper above is still preferred when the model is consumed by ≥ 2 callers (OCP).

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
OdooError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── OdooAuthError               # 401 / 403 / uid=False / AccessDenied / Session Expired
├── OdooAccessError             # ir.model.access / record-rule denial
├── OdooBadRequestError         # 400 / ValidationError / UserError
├── OdooNotFoundError           # 404 / MissingError
├── OdooRateLimitError          # 429 — retry_after_s
└── OdooServerError             # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases:
```
OdooNetworkError = OdooServerError   # legacy name from older code
OdooNotFound     = OdooNotFoundError
```

### The error-inside-200 envelope

Odoo never returns 4xx for ORM-level errors. Every failure arrives as:

```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1,
  "error": {
    "code": -32000,
    "message": "Odoo Server Error" | "Odoo Session Expired",
    "data": {
      "name": "odoo.exceptions.AccessError" | "AccessDenied" | "ValidationError" | ...,
      "message": "…",
      "arguments": [...],
      "debug": "<full traceback>"
    }
  }
}
```

`OdooHTTPClient._classify_error` reads `data.name` and the outer `message` to pick the typed exception:

| Discriminator | Raised |
|---|---|
| `AccessDenied` in `data.name` OR `"Session Expired"` in outer `message` | `OdooAuthError` |
| `AccessError` in `data.name` | `OdooAccessError` |
| `ValidationError` / `UserError` in `data.name` | `OdooBadRequestError` |
| `MissingError` in `data.name` | `OdooNotFoundError` |
| anything else | `OdooError` |

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_post`)

| Condition | Action |
|---|---|
| 401 / 403 / 404 / classified error-in-200 | Raise immediately |
| 503 / 429 | Exponential backoff (`0.5 * 2^attempt` + jitter, max 30s, honours `Retry-After`), up to `max_retries=3` |
| `httpx.ConnectError / ReadError / Timeout` | Same backoff, up to 3 retries, then raise `OdooNetworkError` |
| Mid-flight `OdooAuthError` during `execute_kw` | Cache cleared so the next call re-authenticates; current call still raises |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps them through `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
OdooAuthError      → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
OdooNetworkError   → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
Other OdooError    → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | One of `base_url` / `db` / `username` / `api_key` is blank | Re-enter all four in ACP. The connector validates locally before touching the network. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `common.authenticate` returned `false` — wrong db / user / api_key combination | Triple-check the database name (login-page label, not the URL subdomain), the user login (email), and the API key. Regenerate the API key from Preferences → Account Security if in doubt. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | API key was revoked, or `Odoo Session Expired` returned for the cached uid | The connector already cleared the uid cache and surfaced the error. Generate a new API key and reinstall the connector. |
| `OdooAccessError: You are not allowed to read res.partner` | Logged-in user lacks ACL for the model | Add the user to the matching Odoo security group (e.g. *Sales / Administrator*) in **Settings → Users & Companies → Users**. |
| `OdooBadRequestError: ValidationError` on create_partner | Field passed that the partner form rejects (e.g. malformed email) | Inspect the `response_body['error']['data']['message']` field on the exception — Odoo includes the offending field name. |
| `odoo.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 503 on a shared Odoo.com SaaS instance | The client retries with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| `OdooNetworkError: HTTP 502/504` | Reverse proxy in front of Odoo timed out | Increase `timeout` on `OdooHTTPClient` (default 60s) or move long syncs into a background job. |
| Generated `source_url` opens the wrong record | `base_url` includes a trailing slash or wrong host | The connector strips trailing slashes automatically; verify `base_url` matches the actual Odoo origin (no `/web` suffix). |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'OdooNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `OdooNetworkError = OdooServerError` alias is present in `exceptions.py`. |
